backlog.title: Decide whether Control UI will ever sit behind a reverse proxy
backlog.description: If exposed through a reverse proxy later, set gateway.trustedProxies explicitly. If it stays local-only on loopback, this warning is acceptable.

notes.text: Warren: defer implementation for now, but keep Control UI proxy-compatible. It matters once the UI is internet-exposed, multi-user, or needs centralized auth/TLS/rate limiting.
Mario: defer by default unless Control UI becomes a remotely accessed product surface or shared operator tool. Revisit when public exposure, shared domain routing, or enterprise requirements appear.

backlog.title: Property Manager deferred features
backlog.description: Hold rent collection, accounting, leases, screening, owner financial reporting, vendor marketplace, and broader platform complexity until the streamlined maintenance command center proves value.

notes.text: Barry Bot: deferred feature set documented in PROPERTY_MANAGER_DEFERRED.md so the streamlined v1 can stay sharp.

inProgress.title: Property Manager
inProgress.description: Maintenance command center for small landlords: intake, status, vendor dispatch, tenant updates, and issue history.

notes.text: Barry Bot: elevated as a top active app track. Streamlined v1 concept is documented in PROPERTY_MANAGER_V1.md.
Barry Bot: converted Property Manager into an executable build package with PROPERTY_MANAGER_MVP_SPEC.md and PROPERTY_MANAGER_BUILD_CHECKLIST.md.
Barry Bot: initial property-manager scaffold was created in property-manager/ and committed as fbdfe7c. Boot verification and Slice 1 implementation are now underway.
Barry Bot: Property Manager now boots successfully and core operator pages are live with Prisma-backed data, status transitions, and internal notes in commit 0dde178.
Barry Bot: operator create/edit flows for properties, units, and requests landed in commit fa83e96, and Slice 1 is now under Jeff QA review before Slice 2 begins.

Barry Bot: Jeff cleared Slice 1 for operator scope in commit 0ce1e6b after fixing validation, status-history, and revalidation issues. Slice 2 is now underway for tenant submission, photo upload, and tenant-facing status.
Barry Bot: Bob the Builder is now assigned under Elon as a focused, precise assistant coder to accelerate active implementation work.
Barry Bot: tenant submission, tenant-facing status/timeline, and photo upload landed in commit 58d3d98.
Barry Bot: Bob the Builder added vendor dispatch and vendor queue/detail workflow in commit 25a92ea. Next is Jeff QA on the multi-role flow, then final polish.
Barry Bot: credential-based auth landed in commit 6230b65, replacing the demo role picker with real sign-in and canonical operator/tenant/vendor roles.
Barry Bot: stronger auth failure handling and vendor request permissions landed in commit a83f5be, including dedicated unauthorized routing and server-enforced vendor updates.
Barry Bot: invalid-session 500s were fixed in commit 46221bf by removing cookie mutation from render-time auth checks while preserving fail-closed redirects.

Barry Bot: Jeff cleared the auth/authz slice as PASS WITH CONDITIONS for a controlled private beta checkpoint after confirming protected routes, ownership scoping, visibility boundaries, and invalid-session redirects are materially correct.

Barry Bot: auth boundary integration tests landed in commit 022bb72 covering tampered cookies, expired sessions, tenant object-access denial, vendor unassigned-request denial, and internal-note non-leakage.

Barry Bot: Property Manager now qualifies as a controlled private beta candidate; next step is broader QA, workflow polish, and tighter operator/org scoping rather than pretending it is production-ready.
Barry Bot: sellability path is now documented in PROPERTY_MANAGER_SELLABILITY_CHECKLIST.md with explicit stages for pilot-ready, paid beta-ready, and publicly sellable.
Barry Bot: Property Manager is Chrome-compatible and Windows-compatible as a deployed web app, but it is not a native Windows app or a Chrome extension.
Barry Bot: immediate commercialization priorities are PostgreSQL + deployment, real object storage for photos, tighter operator/org scoping, onboarding/password reset, and billing.

Barry Bot: PostgreSQL + pilot deployment planning landed in PROPERTY_MANAGER_POSTGRES_DEPLOYMENT_PLAN.md via commit 5493a36, with Vercel + Neon + private Cloudflare R2 recommended as the simplest sane pilot stack.
Barry Bot: Bob hardened operator organization scoping in commit 8cb7e16 so operator reads, writes, and request actions no longer trust raw IDs across org boundaries.
Barry Bot: next gate on this slice is Jeff verifying foreign-org access denial and then stabilizing the auth-boundary test harness mismatch on valid-session automated cases.

Barry Bot: rollout direction is now explicit: the operator pays, tenant access is free, vendor access is free, and tenant/vendor accounts should connect into the paying operator workspace via operator-generated invite links or access codes.
Barry Bot: this keeps the buyer and the participant roles clean — operators are the customer, while tenants and vendors are free companion surfaces rather than standalone paid products.

Barry Bot: product direction now includes optional regions inside an operator organization so properties and vendors can be grouped across different towns or service areas without turning v1 into a giant enterprise hierarchy.
Barry Bot: region should start as an organizational/filtering layer first; harder region-based permissions can come later if real use proves they are needed.

Barry Bot: request workflow now explicitly needs visible urgency levels (Low / Medium / High / Emergency) and a separate expected completion date that can be set and updated independently from scheduled date.
Barry Bot: expected completion changes should be traceable in request history so operator planning, vendor coordination, and tenant expectation-setting do not turn into guesswork.
Barry Bot: CSV import/export is now part of product direction, starting with setup/master data import and operational/history export rather than jumping straight into messy ticket-history import.
Barry Bot: full ticket history should be retained in the system, while reporting should default to the last 12 months instead of pretending one-year storage is the right retention limit.
Barry Bot: formalized the streamlined v1 concept in PROPERTY_MANAGER_V1.md and split deferred heavyweight features into a separate holding task.
Mario: start as a maintenance command center for small landlords. Exclude accounting, rent, leases, screening, big owner portals, and feature gravity traps from v1.

inProgress.title: YouTube screensaver channel plan
inProgress.description: Create the action plan and tool stack for 2-10 hour YouTube screensaver videos with ambient music.

notes.text: Barry Bot: finished practical action plan written to YOUTUBE_SCREENSAVER_SOP.md. Core stack: DaVinci Resolve, FFmpeg, Canva, Pexels, Pixabay, YouTube Audio Library, Google Drive, and YouTube Studio. Includes licensing checklist, workflow, recurring cost bands, and what to avoid.
Barry Bot: kicked off Warren review for cheapest viable stack, recurring costs, licensing risks, and what to avoid.

inProgress.title: Finding app ideas
inProgress.description: Find successful apps in Windows Store that do not exist in Apple Store.

notes.text: Barry Bot: kept active as supporting research, but demoted below the current core build tracks now that it has already produced the shortlist and MVP direction.

Barry Bot: second pass complete. Top recommendation is a role-specific clipboard workflow utility. Decision pack written to TOP5_APP_OPPORTUNITIES.md with ranked Top 5 and an MVP spec for the best candidate.

Barry Bot: first concrete shortlist captured in SHORTLIST_APP_IDEAS.md. Strongest buckets: clipboard/text workflow, audio/device switching, file preview/triage, and single-purpose PowerToys-style utilities. Weak buckets: cosmetic customization and commodity archive tools.

Warren: Focus on desktop-control and workflow utilities with recurring pain. Best categories: clipboard/text workflow, file workflow, audio/device switching. Avoid cosmetic-only utilities and broad consumer apps with weak conversion.

Elon: Fastest credible path is a 48-hour sprint: scrape 200-500 Windows candidates across transferable categories, score by reviews/ratings/polish, narrow to 50-75, manually verify Apple absence, then produce a top-10 list with MVP difficulty and monetization angle.

Mario: Don’t just look for anything missing on Apple. Hunt for neglected but valuable workflows. Most interesting gaps are workflow utilities, desktop control/customization, prosumer niche tools, and light enterprise edge cases. Build a shortlist of 20, then score top 5 deeply.
Barry Bot: deduplicated three duplicate backlog cards into this single canonical task and moved it to In Progress.

complete.title: App success
complete.description: Multi-store app acceptance workflow is now covered by Mission Control app workflow tracking, store-account readiness reminders, and the stage-gated process guidance returned by Mario.

notes.text: Barry Bot: marked complete because this now directly supplements the app workflow and store-readiness work already built into Mission Control.

Mario: use a multi-store readiness system with hard stage gates: strategic fit, store viability, compliance complete, submission-ready assets, and launch approval. Design for the strictest store first and standardize metadata/assets in one source of truth.
